payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields are: (1) token_counts (input/output), (2) provider_id, (3) model_id, (4) estimated_price, (5) latency_p50, latency_p90, latency_p95. These enable cost attribution, model tracking, price forecasting, and performance SLA monitoring.
citations: Cost and latency evaluation fixture specification
payload.decision: Document five core accounting dimensions with definitions and measurement scope

entity_resolution.reason: Five distinct accounting fields required for complete eval cost and performance tracking

payload.intent: Explain required evaluation accounting fields for cost and latency tracking in Brain memory system

memory_cards.statement: Token counts measure input and output tokens consumed per API call, tracked separately for prompt and completion
Provider and model IDs identify the service endpoint and specific model variant used for reproducibility and cost attribution
Estimated price derives from token counts and provider pricing schedules, enabling cost tracking and budget forecasting
Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) measure response time distribution, with p50 as median, p90 and p95 capturing tail latency

details: Specified measurement scope for each field
Established relationships between token counts and pricing
